svg_graph-gpt5-plane-5-P1503_4-7a427701-4/svg_graph-gpt5-plane-5-P1503_4-7a427701-4.png
high_level
A time-scaled Keystroke-Level Model timeline that visualizes sequential Mental, Keystroke, Pointing, and Homing operations as colored blocks whose widths reflect their durations.
mid_level
The image features a title reading "Keystroke-Level Model (time-scaled)" at the top left and a compact legend showing four colored swatches labeled "M (Mental)", "K (Keystroke)", "P (Pointing)", and "H (Homing)". Below the legend a horizontal time axis runs across the lower portion of the graphic with tick marks and labels from 0s to 6s at one-second intervals. Above that axis a single row of rounded rectangular blocks is placed left to right representing the sequence of operations: an orange/yellow "M 1.35s" block, a short blue "K" block, a green "P 1.1s" block, a purple "H" block, two additional short blue "K" blocks, another green "P 1.1s" block, and a final orange/yellow "M 1.35s" block; block widths are visually proportional to their stated durations and all share the same height and centered labels.
low_level (2003 chars)
[expand]
Centered around a clear title in the upper left that reads "Keystroke-Level Model (time-scaled)", the visual is organized into a legend, a horizontal time axis, and a row of time-scaled operation blocks. The legend, positioned beneath the title, contains four small square color swatches with labels: a warm orange/yellow labeled "M (Mental)", a pale sky blue labeled "K (Keystroke)", a light mint green labeled "P (Pointing)", and a soft lavender labeled "H (Homing)". A bold horizontal axis stretches across the lower region of the image with short vertical tick marks at regular intervals and numerical labels directly beneath them reading "0s", "1s", "2s", "3s", "4s", "5s", and "6s", indicating equally spaced one-second increments. Directly above this axis is a single aligned row of rounded rectangles that represent the chronological sequence of actions: starting at the left is an orange/yellow block labeled "M 1.35s" that is noticeably wide, followed by a narrow blue block labeled "K", then a substantially wider green block labeled "P 1.1s", then a medium-width purple block labeled "H", then two more narrow blue blocks each labeled "K", then another green block labeled "P 1.1s" of the same wider size, and finally a wide orange/yellow block labeled "M 1.35s" at the rightmost end. All blocks share the same vertical size and rounded corners, each has a slightly darker colored outline matching its fill, and the text labels are centered horizontally within each block. The composition emphasizes proportional duration by block width, establishes a left-to-right temporal flow aligned to the second-marked axis, and uses consistent color and typographic labeling to map blocks to the legend entries and to make the sequence of mental, keystroke, pointing, and homing operations immediately legible.
metadata
persona: A meticulous human-computer interaction specialist studying the relation between humans and computer systems on a granular level
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: plane
description: A Keystroke-Level Model (KLM) timeline: operation blocks (Mental, Keystroke, Pointing, Homing) placed on a time axis with proportional durations.
svg_graph-gpt5-biomechanics-6-P8825_14-e43673b0-4/svg_graph-gpt5-biomechanics-6-P8825_14-e43673b0-4.png
high_level
Centered diagram titled "Spine Alignment" that compares a neutral (teal, dashed) spine curve and a flexed/slouched (solid red) spine curve against a vertical plumb line for posture coaching.
mid_level
A clean white diagram with the title "Spine Alignment" centered at the top. A vertical, dashed light-gray plumb line runs down the canvas and is labeled "Plumb line" slightly to its right. Two stylized spine profiles run alongside the plumb line: a dashed teal curve with small teal circular markers representing a neutral alignment close to the plumb line, and a solid red curve with red circular markers showing a more forward-slouched, flexed posture offset away from the plumb line. A pale circular head hint sits near the top of the curves and a rounded rectangular pelvis hint sits near the bottom; a small legend in the lower-left uses colored swatches and the labels "Neutral" and "Flexed" to identify the lines.
low_level (1507 chars)
[expand]
On a white background a centered dark-gray title reads "Spine Alignment" at the top, beneath which a vertical dashed light-gray plumb line runs from near the top toward the bottom center of the image; the short label "Plumb line" appears to the right of the line about one tenth of the way down. Two curved spine silhouettes are drawn adjacent to the plumb line: a teal curve made of medium-thick short dashes that stays relatively close to the plumb line and is punctuated by a series of small filled teal circles at regular intervals representing vertebrae from neck through lower back, and a solid coral-red curve that begins slightly left of the teal line and bends forward more prominently away from the plumb line, also marked by small filled red circles at corresponding spinal levels. Near the top of the curves a pale, lightly stroked circular head shape hints at skull position slightly left of center, and a pale rounded rectangle with a subtle border at the base of the curves indicates the pelvis area roughly centered under the plumb line. A concise legend sits in the lower-left corner with a short teal rectangular swatch labeled "Neutral" and a short red swatch labeled "Flexed," both labels rendered in a muted dark-gray sans-serif; overall the visual hierarchy uses muted grays for labels and background elements and bright teal and red for the two spine states so the neutral and flexed alignments read clearly against the central plumb reference.
metadata
persona: A UI/UX designer who appreciates the puzzle solver's input on creating intuitive user interfaces
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: biomechanics
description: Spine alignment comparison: neutral vs flexed (slouched) curve against a plumb line, useful for posture coaching UI.
svg_graph-gpt5-functions-8-P3283_15-5b86ddb8-3/svg_graph-gpt5-functions-8-P3283_15-5b86ddb8-3.png
high_level
A clean semicircular "Applause Meter" gauge with a needle angled into the green High zone to indicate strong audience energy.
mid_level
Centered under a pale sky-blue background is the title Applause Meter and below it a large semicircular gauge made of three thick color bands: a red arc on the left for Low, a yellow arc in the middle for Medium, and a green arc on the right for High. Thin grey tick marks radiate around the arc at regular intervals, and a small dark pivot sits at the bottom center from which a rounded dark needle extends upward and to the right, resting in the green zone (about 70%). A muted grey label beneath the gauge reads Low Medium High aligned under the corresponding arc zones.
low_level (1345 chars)
[expand]
The image displays a minimalist backstage-style applause meter on a very pale sky-blue rectangular backdrop. At the top center, a dark navy title reads Applause Meter in a clean sans-serif style. Dominating the composition is a large semicircular gauge that arcs from the lower-left to the lower-right of the image; the arc is rendered as three contiguous thick bands: a leftmost red segment representing Low, a central yellow segment representing Medium, and a rightmost green segment representing High. Around the arc there are eleven thin, evenly spaced grey tick marks that fan out from left to right, including a top-center tick and identical end ticks at the extreme left and right. The gauge’s pivot is a small dark circular knob located near the bottom center of the arc; from that pivot a solid, rounded-capped dark needle shoots upward and to the right, cutting across the yellow band and pointing well into the green band, indicating roughly seventy percent toward High. Beneath the gauge, centered and in a muted grey, the words Low Medium High are aligned horizontally to label the three colored zones. The overall visual hierarchy emphasizes the thick colored arcs and the dark needle, with supportive thin tick marks and understated labels to make it easy to read audience energy at a glance.
metadata
persona: A retired television producer who closely follows their sibling's career on stage
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: functions
description: An applause meter gauge used backstage to visualize audience energy, with ticks and a needle.
table  317,563 (source 318,071) samples
HTML-rendered tables with dense annotations
65 GB source / 33 GB tars · /weka/oe-training-default/mm-olmo/molmo3_datasets/text_rich_caption/table
3 random samples shown
html_doc-gpt5-hierarchical-5-P9580_10-412d08c7-2/html_doc-gpt5-hierarchical-5-P9580_10-412d08c7-2.png
high_level
A dark-themed compliance matrix card showing expandable UK regions and their customs, systems, risk levels, and port-specific remarks for French flour exports.
mid_level
Centered on a deep blue radial-gradient background is a rounded card titled "Compliance Matrix • FR to UK Regions" with a small subtitle and a bright pill badge reading "Prepared for: Flour Exports." The main content is a five-column table with column headers: Region / Expand, Core Requirement, Systems, Risk Level, and Remarks. Each row represents a UK nation (England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland) and includes an interactive chevron and a small colored tag that summarizes regional notes; each region can be expanded to reveal a nested subtable listing steps (for example customs declaration, commodity origin, safety & security), associated systems (CDS, ENS, Driver Docs, etc.), timing (pre-arrival, pre-lodgement), and port-specific notes (Dover, Felixstowe, Cairnryan, Holyhead, Belfast/Larne). Risk levels are visually highlighted (green for low, amber for moderate) and system names appear in compact monospaced-style labels.
low_level (3911 chars)
[expand]
A single centered rectangular card with softly rounded corners and a subtle drop shadow sits against a dark navy-to-deep-teal radial gradient background. At the top-left of the card is a bold title that reads "Compliance Matrix • FR to UK Regions" and directly beneath it a muted smaller line: "Expand a region to view customs declarations, notifications, and port notes." At the top-right is a bright, rounded badge in a green gradient labeled "Prepared for: Flour Exports." Below this header is a full-width table with five column headers in a lighter muted tone: "Region / Expand", "Core Requirement", "Systems", "Risk Level", and "Remarks." Each table row is separated by a faint spacing and sits on a slightly lighter translucent band with a thin border; the first column of each row contains an interactive chevron icon followed by the region name in heavy type and a small pill-shaped tag with an accompanying tiny graphical mark and short descriptor (England: "High throughput" with a circular green dot; Scotland: "Ferries" with a square icon; Wales: "West routes" with a horizontal mark; Northern Ireland: "Specific processes" with an amber circle). The main row cells display concise entries: England lists "Customs declaration" under Core Requirement, a compact monospaced-style label "CDS" under Systems, a green "Low with preparation" risk indicator, and the remark "Use pre-lodged MRN for rapid flow." Scotland shows "Pre-lodgement," system "CDS," risk "Low," and "Ensure documents with driver." Wales shows "Doc pack," system "Driver," risk "Low," and "Keep digital copies." Northern Ireland shows "Movement guidance," system "Service," an amber "Moderate" risk label, and the remark "Confirm route and paperwork." Each region row is designed to expand: when expanded a nested subpanel appears to the right of the region name separated by a subtle dashed vertical rule, containing its own compact table with column headings "Step," "System," "When," and "Port Notes." The nested rows enumerate detailed steps: for England these include "Customs declaration" (System: CDS, When: Pre-arrival, Port Notes: "Dover, Felixstowe: check pre-lodgement."), "Commodity code & origin" (CDS, Pre-lodgement, "Maintain supporting documents for preference."), and "Safety & security (if required)" (ENS, Before departure, "Carrier-led; confirm with haulier."); for Scotland the nested items list "Customs declaration" (CDS, Pre-arrival, "Cairnryan: coordinate with ferry operator.") and "Labelling check" (Internal QC, Pre-dispatch, "Language/weights compliant."); for Wales they list "Customs declaration" (CDS, Pre-arrival, "Holyhead: ensure vehicle reference with ferry booking.") and "Document pack" (Driver Docs, At loading, "Commercial invoice, packing list, origin statement."); and for Northern Ireland the nested items show "Customs support" (Support Service, Pre-arrival, "Belfast/Larne: follow NI movement guidance.") and "Commodity & origin" (Declaration, Pre-lodgement, "Preference rules may apply."). Visual details include small simple vector icons next to chevrons and port notes, monospaced rounded labels for system names, dashed separators and subtly rounded first and last cells, muted column header typography, and color-coded status text (soft green for OK/low, warm amber for moderate) to create a clear hierarchy of information for verifying procedural steps before dispatch.
metadata
persona: A French flour exporter struggling to navigate new trade regulations post-Brexit
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: hierarchical
description: A regional compliance tree: expand each UK nation to see customs and logistics milestones, systems, and port-specific considerations (grouped by region). Helps verify procedural steps before dispatch.
latex_doc-gpt5-confusion-9-P7758_18-d44fd35b-7/latex_doc-gpt5-confusion-9-P7758_18-d44fd35b-7.png
high_level
A no-nonsense confusion matrix summarising bushfire risk classifications for paddocks into High, Moderate and Low categories, with accuracy and recall metrics.
mid_level
Centered at the top is the bold title "Bushfire Risk — 3-Class Confusion Matrix" with a small note below reading "Classes: High, Moderate, Low. Emphasis on catching High risk." Below that sits a three-by-three table: column headers are "Pred. High", "Pred. Mod.", and "Pred. Low" and row headers are "Actual High", "Actual Moderate", and "Actual Low." The diagonal cells of 41, 57 and 72 are highlighted in a muted green to show correct predictions, while off-diagonal misclassifications are shown in pale to stronger red shades. A summary line underneath reports "Overall Accuracy 84.7% High Recall 85.4% Paddock-days: 200."
low_level (1937 chars)
[expand]
A simple, practical page dominated by a centered heading that reads "Bushfire Risk — 3-Class Confusion Matrix" in a larger font, with a smaller line underneath that states "Classes: High, Moderate, Low. Emphasis on catching High risk." Directly beneath this title is a rectangular table with a clear three-column by three-row layout. The top column header cells are shaded a warm amber and labeled, left to right, "Pred. High", "Pred. Mod.", and "Pred. Low." The leftmost column of row labels also has a pale amber background and lists, from top to bottom, "Actual High", "Actual Moderate", and "Actual Low." The interior grid shows numeric counts: the top-left cell (Actual High / Pred. High) is a bold "41" set against a muted gumleaf green background to indicate a correct high-risk prediction; to its right are "5" (Pred. Mod.) in a light red tint and "2" (Pred. Low) in a slightly stronger red tint indicating misclassifications. The middle row (Actual Moderate) reads "6" under Pred. High in a light red, "57" under Pred. Mod. in bold gumleaf green, and "7" under Pred. Low in light red. The bottom row (Actual Low) shows "3" under Pred. High with a darker red wash, "8" under Pred. Mod. in light red, and "72" under Pred. Low in bold gumleaf green. The table is framed with a pronounced ochre-toned rule at the top and bottom, and the overall color scheme uses earthy tones—green for correct cells, amber for headers, and varying reds for errors—giving a rugged, utilitarian feel. Below the table, aligned on the same line, is a compact summary in plain text: "Overall Accuracy 84.7% High Recall 85.4% Paddock-days: 200." The layout leaves generous white margins around the centered content and emphasizes clarity and readability over decoration.
metadata
persona: An Australian cowboy who values practicality and durability over all else.
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: confusion
description: Bushfire risk alert across paddocks: High, Moderate, Low classes for task prioritisation and safety.
plotly_chart-gpt5-financial-2-P6828_15-504422cc-2/plotly_chart-gpt5-financial-2-P6828_15-504422cc-2.png
high_level
A titled planning table that recommends price raises, keeps, or markdowns per SKU using elasticity to forecast changes in units, revenue, and profit and to highlight profit deltas.
mid_level
The image is a wide, spreadsheet-style table titled "SKU Price-Action Planner (Elasticity-Driven Profit Impact)" placed at the top left. Column headers across the top identify SKU, current price, unit cost, four-week units, elasticity, proposed price change, new price, forecast units, forecast revenue, forecast profit, profit delta versus current, and the recommended action. Eight product rows list SKUs such as "4K TV 55in" and "Cordless Vacuum," followed by a final totals row labeled "TOTAL PLAN IMPACT." Numeric columns are right-aligned, prices are shown with two decimals, revenues and profits are displayed as whole-dollar amounts, and the profit-delta column is color-coded green for gains, red for losses and white for neutral; action cells are lightly shaded green for raises, pale yellow for markdowns, and white for keeps.
low_level (3276 chars)
[expand]
A large rectangular table fills the image under a left-aligned title reading "SKU Price-Action Planner (Elasticity-Driven Profit Impact)" in a darker headline color. The header row has a light gray background with centered dark blue text and column titles: "SKU", "Current Price", "Unit Cost", "Units (4 wks)", "Elasticity |e|", "Proposed Price Δ", "New Price", "Forecast Units", "Forecast Revenue", "Forecast Profit", "Profit Δ vs Current", and "Action". The first eight data rows list individual products in the leftmost, wider column: "4K TV 55in", "Cordless Vacuum", "Basic Tee 3-Pack", "Organic Coffee 1lb", "Vitamin C Serum", "Bluetooth Earbuds", "Air Fryer 5qt", and "Kids Building Set". For each SKU the table shows a current price (two decimals) and unit cost (two decimals): $449.99 / $360.00 for the 4K TV, $179.99 / $105.00 for the Cordless Vacuum, $24.99 / $10.00 for the Basic Tee, $8.49 / $6.50 for the coffee, $22.99 / $10.50 for the serum, $59.99 / $28.00 for the earbuds, $99.99 / $60.00 for the air fryer, and $29.99 / $16.00 for the kids set. Four-week unit volumes are shown as integers (260, 420, 1,200, 2,100, 760, 980, 620) and elasticities are shown to two decimals (0.85, 1.10, 1.35, 1.60, 1.25, 1.05, 0.90, 1.45). The proposed price change column displays the percent adjustments as "+6%", "+0%", or "-8%" according to elasticity rules, producing new prices: $476.99, $179.99, $22.99, $7.81, $21.15, $59.99, $105.99, and $27.59 respectively. Forecast units after the price move appear as 247, 420, 1,330, 2,369, 836, 980, 511, and 692. Forecast revenue is shown as whole-dollar amounts: $117,817; $75,596; $30,577; $18,502; $17,681; $58,790; $54,161; and $19,092. Forecast profit (whole dollars) is displayed as $28,897; $31,496; $17,277; $3,103; $8,903; $31,350; $23,501; and $8,020. The profit delta column compares forecast profit to current profit and is color-coded: positive deltas have a light green background, negatives a light red background, and zeros remain white; the deltas read +$5,499, $0, -$711, -$1,076, -$589, $0, +$1,906, and -$654. The rightmost action column lists the recommended action for each row — "Raise", "Keep", or "Markdown" — with a light-green background for "Raise", pale yellow for "Markdown" and white for "Keep"; the specific recommendations are: Raise (4K TV), Keep (Cordless Vacuum), Markdown (Basic Tee), Markdown (Organic Coffee), Markdown (Vitamin C Serum), Keep (Bluetooth Earbuds), Raise (Air Fryer), and Markdown (Kids Building Set). The final row uses "TOTAL PLAN IMPACT" in the SKU column, dashes in most numeric columns, and shows a summed profit delta of $4,376 highlighted in the delta color for a positive outcome; the action cell in the totals row is a neutral dash. Numeric columns are right-aligned for easy scanning, SKU names are left-aligned, and the table uses restrained styling and subtle background fills to emphasize header labels and the color-coded profit and action signals.
metadata
persona: A cost-conscious retailer who prioritizes profit margins and customer demand over supplier ethics
model_name: gpt-5
content_type: financial
description: SKU Price-Action Planner — models price changes using demand elasticity to forecast units, revenue, and profit, highlighting profit deltas to decide raises or markdowns for each SKU.
visual_knowledge_data_dump  546,986 entities / ~2.3M training samples samples
Allen AI entity QA dump (S3 explore-multimodal-datasets/data-dumps/entities/) — named entity + question + answer + candidate image URLs. Packed ~2.3M (entity, image, answer) training samples (5 imgs/entity × ~460k entities w/ valid imgs; 0.2% PNGs corrupt → skipped)
25 GB JSON + 1.6 TB images + 4.7 TB tars · /weka/oe-training-default/oe-encoder/visual_knowledge_data_dump
15 random samples shown
5 images
entities-0.json · df26036f-6aa7-4bc5-854e-7e6c9a2f08d2
namedEntity
Sooglossidae
question
what kind of frog is this
answer
This is an image of a Sooglossidae frog. These frogs, also known as Seychelles frogs or Seychelles Island frogs, are a unique family found exclusively in the Seychelles Islands. They're quite small, typically around 4 cm in length, and have some interesting characteristics. For example, they lay their eggs on moist ground rather than in water, and some species even skip the tadpole stage entirely, with their eggs hatching directly into tiny froglets.
97 images
entities-0.json · c00d41a2-f633-4d4c-994f-e5cf4669753d
namedEntity
Mikel Arteta
question
What clubs has this person played for?
answer
This is an image of Mikel Arteta. Mikel Arteta has played for several clubs throughout his career. He started at Barcelona's youth academy, La Masia, before moving to Paris Saint-Germain on loan. He then had spells with Rangers in Scotland and Real Sociedad in Spain. Arteta is perhaps best known for his time in the English Premier League, where he played for Everton for six and a half seasons before finishing his playing career at Arsenal.
77 images
entities-0.json · 17cb66b7-bc39-4061-b27d-ecede3682f28
namedEntity
Maltese dog
question
are they prone to any diseases?
answer
Maltese dogs, while generally healthy, can be prone to certain health conditions. They may experience issues with their liver and heart, as well as a condition called luxating patella, which affects the knee joint. Additionally, they should be monitored for patent ductus arteriosus, a heart defect. Despite these potential concerns, Maltese dogs are known for their longevity, with recent studies showing they have a life expectancy of around 13 years, which is slightly above average for purebred dogs.
304 images
entities-0.json · 13d730ee-acc6-4c34-a48c-9ea78a1f9161
namedEntity
Hulk Hogan
question
who is tis
answer
This is Hulk Hogan. He is a legendary professional wrestler widely regarded as one of the most recognized wrestling stars worldwide and the most popular wrestler of the 1980s. Hulk Hogan, whose real name is Terry Gene Bollea, began his professional wrestling career in 1977 and gained worldwide recognition after signing with the World Wrestling Federation (now WWE) in 1983. He became the face of professional wrestling during the 1980s wrestling boom, headlining multiple WrestleMania events and becoming a cultural icon known for his signature red and yellow attire, handlebar mustache, and catchphrases.
105 images
entities-0.json · 6b96c413-594a-479c-b343-ddfa7e45afdb
namedEntity
Michael Collins (astronaut)
question
what is this person known for? did he do anything notable?
answer
This is an image of Michael Collins, the American astronaut. Michael Collins is best known for his role as the command module pilot on the historic Apollo 11 mission, the first lunar landing. While his crewmates Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the Moon's surface, Collins orbited alone in the command module Columbia. Collins made significant contributions to space exploration beyond Apollo 11. He flew on Gemini 10, performing two spacewalks and becoming the first person to perform multiple spacewalks on a single mission. After his NASA career, Collins served as the Assistant Secretary of State for Public Affairs and later became the director of the National Air and Space Museum, overseeing its construction and opening in 1976. His accomplishments in spaceflight, public service, and museum leadership make Michael Collins a notable figure in American history and space exploration.
1 images
entities-0.json · 759ff610-16e1-4761-b50b-a314500bcf50
namedEntity
Ukrainian National Chernobyl Museum
question
How many lines of cyrilic print?
answer
This is an image of the Ukrainian National Chernobyl Museum sign. At the center of the image, there are three lines of Cyrillic print. These lines likely display the museum's name in Ukrainian, which is the official language of Ukraine where the museum is located. The Cyrillic script is commonly used for writing Ukrainian, and its presence on the museum's sign is appropriate given the museum's focus on the Chernobyl disaster, a significant event in Ukrainian history.

namedEntity
Harvest Festival (United Kingdom)
question
when is the best time to harvest these?
answer
This is an image of the Harvest Festival in the United Kingdom. The best time to harvest crops for the Harvest Festival typically varies depending on local traditions and the types of crops grown in each area. Generally, harvests take place in September or October, as winter approaches. The exact timing is flexible to accommodate different harvest schedules across the country, allowing churches and communities to celebrate once their local crops are ready.

entities-0.json · 8a3e2c0c-8c81-413f-b24d-e9a82783a8f8
namedEntity
Tim Curry
question
How many shows was Tim Curry in?
answer
Tim Curry is a prolific actor with an extensive career spanning theater, film, television, and voice acting. While it's difficult to provide an exact count of all the shows he's been in, his career highlights include: Theater: Curry has starred in numerous stage productions, including "Hair," "The Rocky Horror Show," "Amadeus," and "Spamalot." Television: He's appeared in many TV series and miniseries, with notable roles in "Wiseguy," "Earth 2," "Stephen King's It," and voice work in animated shows like "Peter Pan and the Pirates" and "The Wild Thornberrys." Film: Curry has featured in many movies, such as "The Rocky Horror Picture Show," "Clue," "Legend," and "Home Alone 2: Lost in New York." Voice acting: He's lent his voice to numerous animated series, films, and video games. Overall, Tim Curry has been in dozens of shows across various mediums throughout his long and diverse career.
127 images
entities-0.json · 00a963ec-fcdd-4d44-bd97-997684bddeae
namedEntity
Ernst & Young
question
What is the work culture like here?
answer
Ernst & Young, also known as EY, has a complex work culture with both positive and challenging aspects. While the company has been consistently recognized as one of the best places to work by Fortune magazine for over 25 years, there have been some concerning issues reported. The work environment at EY can be demanding, with long hours being a significant concern. Some employees have reported routinely working over 51 hours per week, with some even exceeding 61 hours. This has led to health concerns and high turnover rates among staff. There have also been reports of workplace issues such as bullying, sexual harassment, and racism affecting a portion of employees. Additionally, a controversial training program for women employees received criticism for promoting outdated and sexist ideas. On the positive side, EY has shown willingness to address these issues by commissioning independent culture reviews and implementing recommendations for improvement. The company's consistent ranking as a top employer suggests that many employees still find value in working there, despite the challenges. Overall, the work culture at EY appears to be a mix of professional opportunities and workplace pressures, with ongoing efforts to address and improve problematic areas.
12 images
entities-0.json · 9a207a4e-32a5-45ed-9791-86d763dd62a8
namedEntity
Lemon pepper wings
question
What are the main ingredients of the lemon pepper wing
answer
Lemon pepper wings are a popular dish, particularly in Atlanta cuisine. The main ingredients for these wings are: 1. Chicken wings 2. Lemon pepper seasoning (typically containing lemon zest, black pepper, and sometimes sugar) The wings are usually unbreaded and roasted before being coated with the lemon pepper seasoning. In some variations, like "lemon pepper wet," the wings may also be tossed in a lemon sauce or butter for added flavor.

Kim Ji-woong (entertainer)
question
What is his group's debut song?
answer
This is an image of Kim Ji-woong, a member of the South Korean boy band Zerobaseone. The group's debut song was "In Bloom," which was released as part of their first mini album "Youth in the Shade" in July 2023 when they officially debuted as Zerobaseone.

entities-0.json · f7805f83-ff2e-4aee-898f-929a0d59d902
namedEntity
Adetomyrma venatrix
question
what do they drink?
answer
Adetomyrma venatrix, also known as the Dracula ant, drinks the blood of its young. This unusual species, endemic to Madagascar, has a grisly feeding habit that gives it its vampire-like nickname. The worker ants consume the hemolymph (insect blood) of their own larvae, a behavior that is quite unique in the ant world.
28 images
entities-0.json · b66e6d67-fcbf-45bd-8300-3e96e894ac84
namedEntity
Park Jin-young
question
Who is this person?
answer
This is an image of Park Jin-young, also known by his stage names J. Y. Park and The Asiansoul. He's a prominent figure in the South Korean entertainment industry, recognized as a singer-songwriter, record producer, and the founder of JYP Entertainment, one of South Korea's most successful entertainment agencies. Park rose to fame with his 1994 debut album "Blue City" and has since played a crucial role in developing and managing many successful K-pop artists.

entities-0.json · 3466fa46-dc15-4d8f-ad4e-d56cade011d5
namedEntity
Butter cookie
question
Where did these cookies originate?
answer
These are butter cookies, also known as Danish butter cookies. They originated in Denmark. These crisp, buttery cookies are made with a simple combination of butter, flour, and sugar, giving them their signature rich flavor and texture. Denmark has been exporting these delicious treats for many years, particularly to the United States and Asia, making them a popular international delicacy.

entities-0.json · 6b6fb645-f22c-4f1c-a01c-0dab66fef829
namedEntity
Gaara
question
Who did he fight in the second stage of the Chunin exam?
answer
This question is about Sasuke Uchiha from the Naruto anime and manga series. In the second stage of the Chunin exam, Sasuke fought against Gaara of the Desert. This was a significant matchup, as Gaara was a formidable opponent known for his ability to manipulate sand and his ruthless fighting style. The battle between Sasuke and Gaara was a key moment in the Chunin exams, showcasing Sasuke's growth as a ninja and setting the stage for future conflicts involving Gaara.
